0.q: Transgenic tobacco plants overexpressing Oryza (OsINO1) as well as Porteresia (PcINO1) cytosolic L-myo-inositol-1-phosphate synthase coding genes can withstand and retain their chromosomal and DNA integrity in 100 mM NaCl solution.
A direct correlation between unabated increased synthesis of inositol under salinity stress by the PcINO1 gene product and salt tolerance has been demonstrated for all the systems.

1.q: stressed roots of transgenic plants invariably accumulated higher Na+ and K+ ion contents compared to roots of untransformed plants; whereas, shoots of transgenics accumulated lower levels of both the ions, clearly suggesting the involvement of PcSrp in ion homeostasis

2.q: shoots of transgenics accumulated lower levels of both the ions than that of untransformed plants under identical stress, clearly suggesting the involvement of PcSrp in ion homeostasis contributing to salt tolerance.

3.q: introgression of the PcINO1 gene coding for a salt-tolerant MIPS protein (L-myo-inositol 1-phosphate synthase) from Porteresia coarctata in tobacco plants makes them capable of synthesizing inositol even in presence of high salt concentration

4.q: the salt-tolerant myo-inositol phosphate synthase (MIPS) coding gene (PcINO1) producing inositol

5.q: the salt-tolerant myo-inositol phosphate synthase (MIPS) coding gene (PcINO1) producing inositol
